entries.purpose: Central orchestration agent. Subscribes to APPL_DB tables (routes, neighbors, ports, VLANs, ACLs, tunnels) and translates each desired-state entry into SAI API calls written to ASIC_DB. Syncd consumes ASIC_DB to drive the vendor SDK. Orchagent is the single point of ASIC programming for all SONiC features.
entries.restart_warning: Restarting swss tears down and rebuilds all SAI state. Causes a brief forwarding interruption while orchagent replays APPL_DB into ASIC_DB. On platforms without Warm Reboot, this resets hardware forwarding tables.

entries.purpose: SAI-to-SDK bridge. Consumes ASIC_DB entries written by orchagent and calls the vendor SAI implementation (libsai.so) to program the forwarding ASIC. Also polls ASIC counters via the flex-counter framework and publishes them to COUNTERS_DB. Vendor-specific; each platform ships its own libsai.
entries.restart_warning: Restarting syncd without Warm Reboot causes a hardware forwarding reset. All programmed SAI objects are destroyed; orchagent must replay the full ASIC_DB to restore state. Traffic loss for the duration of the replay.

entries.purpose: FPM (Forwarding Plane Manager) synchronization daemon. Receives RIB updates from FRR's zebra process via the FPM socket (Unix socket, default port 2620) and writes them as ROUTE_TABLE entries into APPL_DB. Bridges the FRR routing stack and the SONiC forwarding pipeline.
entries.restart_warning: Restarting the bgp container restarts FRR (bgpd, zebra, staticd) and fpmsyncd together. BGP sessions will drop; routes will be withdrawn and re-installed after reconvergence.

entries.purpose: BGP configuration daemon. Watches CONFIG_DB for BGP neighbor, peer-group, prefix-list, route-map, and global ASN changes and translates them into FRR vtysh configuration commands applied live. Ensures FRR configuration stays in sync with CONFIG_DB without requiring a BGP container restart.
entries.restart_warning: Restarting bgp container drops all BGP sessions. FRR will re-read its startup config and bgpcfgd will replay CONFIG_DB entries. Expect full BGP reconvergence delay.

entries.purpose: Port synchronization daemon. Reads port configuration (speed, MTU, admin state, FEC mode, auto-negotiation) from CONFIG_DB and writes desired state to APPL_DB:PORT_TABLE. Also monitors netlink events for kernel-level link state changes and publishes them to STATE_DB:PORT_TABLE, gating other daemons that wait for port readiness.
entries.restart_warning: Restarting swss restarts the entire swss container including orchagent. See orchagent restart warning.

entries.purpose: Neighbor synchronization daemon. Monitors the kernel ARP/NDP table via netlink NEIGH events and writes resolved neighbor entries (IP-to-MAC mappings) to APPL_DB:NEIGH_TABLE. Orchagent converts these into SAI neighbor and next-hop objects in ASIC_DB.
entries.restart_warning: Restarting swss flushes APPL_DB neighbor entries. Hardware ARP/NDP table is rebuilt from kernel state after restart. Expect brief traffic blackholing for connected hosts while neighbors are re-resolved.

entries.purpose: Port manager daemon. Subscribes to CONFIG_DB:PORT table and applies port configuration to the Linux kernel (via netlink/ip commands): sets MTU, admin state (ip link set up/down), and interface description. Counterpart to portsyncd; portmgrd handles the kernel side while portsyncd handles the SAI/APPL_DB side.
entries.restart_warning: Restarting swss restarts the entire swss container. See orchagent restart warning.

entries.purpose: Interface manager daemon. Subscribes to CONFIG_DB:INTERFACE and CONFIG_DB:VLAN_INTERFACE tables and applies IP address assignments to Linux kernel interfaces. Writes interface entries to APPL_DB for orchagent to create the corresponding RIF (Router Interface) SAI objects. Handles IPv4 and IPv6 address add/delete events.
entries.restart_warning: Restarting swss restarts the entire swss container. See orchagent restart warning.

entries.purpose: VLAN manager daemon. Subscribes to CONFIG_DB:VLAN and CONFIG_DB:VLAN_MEMBER tables and creates/destroys Linux VLAN bridge interfaces and bridge FDB entries. Writes VLAN and VLAN member entries to APPL_DB so orchagent can create the corresponding SAI VLAN objects and port membership.
entries.restart_warning: Restarting swss restarts the entire swss container. See orchagent restart warning.

entries.purpose: Team (LAG/PortChannel) manager daemon. Subscribes to CONFIG_DB:PORTCHANNEL and CONFIG_DB:PORTCHANNEL_MEMBER tables and manages the teamd (libteam) process lifecycle: starts/stops a teamd instance per PortChannel and adds/removes member ports. Writes LAG state to APPL_DB:LAG_TABLE for orchagent.
entries.restart_warning: Restarting the teamd container causes all LACP sessions to drop momentarily. PortChannel interfaces will briefly go down; traffic on LAG members will fall back to individual links or blackhole depending on upstream switch behavior.

entries.purpose: Team synchronization daemon. Subscribes to LAG state changes from the teamd processes (via libteamdctl) and publishes LAG member port state (selected/deselected by LACP) to APPL_DB:LAG_MEMBER_TABLE. Orchagent uses this to add or remove ports from the SAI LAG object.
entries.restart_warning: See teammgrd restart warning. teamsyncd runs in the same container.

entries.purpose: LLDP synchronization daemon. Polls the lldpd process for neighbor information (via lldpcli) and publishes discovered LLDP neighbor entries to APPL_DB:LLDP_ENTRY_TABLE. These entries are used by the topology discovery CLI and optionally by lldpmgrd for interface description auto-population.
entries.restart_warning: Restarting the lldp container causes lldpd to restart, dropping all active LLDP sessions. Neighbors will take up to one tx-interval (default 30s) to be re-learned.

entries.purpose: LLDP manager daemon. Subscribes to CONFIG_DB:DEVICE_METADATA and CONFIG_DB:PORT tables and configures the lldpd process accordingly: sets the chassis ID, system description, and per-port LLDP parameters (admin status, tx/rx enable). Translates SONiC CONFIG_DB intent into lldpcli commands.
entries.restart_warning: See lldpsyncd restart warning. lldpmgrd runs in the same container.

entries.purpose: Transceiver daemon. Manages pluggable optics and DAC cables: reads EEPROM data (vendor, part number, serial, DOM measurements) from SFP/QSFP/OSFP modules via the platform SFP API and publishes it to STATE_DB:TRANSCEIVER_INFO and STATE_DB:TRANSCEIVER_DOM_SENSOR tables. Also handles module initialization sequences and high-power module power-up state machines.
entries.restart_warning: Restarting pmon restarts all platform monitoring daemons (xcvrd, psud, thermalctld, syseepromd). DOM and transceiver state will be briefly unavailable. No forwarding impact; purely monitoring.

entries.purpose: Neighbor manager daemon. Manages static ARP/NDP entries and neighbor probing from CONFIG_DB. Writes neighbor entries to kernel via netlink.
entries.restart_warning: Restarts entire swss container. See orchagent restart warning.

entries.purpose: Buffer manager daemon. Translates CONFIG_DB buffer pool/profile/PG/queue configuration into APPL_DB entries for orchagent to program SAI buffer objects.
entries.restart_warning: Restarts entire swss container.

entries.purpose: CoPP (Control Plane Policing) manager daemon. Reads COPP_TABLE from CONFIG_DB and writes CoPP trap/policer entries to APPL_DB for orchagent to program in SAI.
entries.restart_warning: Restarts entire swss container.

entries.purpose: Tunnel manager daemon. Manages VXLAN tunnel configuration from CONFIG_DB and writes tunnel entries to APPL_DB for orchagent VxlanTunnelOrch.
entries.restart_warning: Restarts entire swss container.

entries.purpose: sFlow manager daemon. Manages sFlow sampling configuration from CONFIG_DB.
entries.restart_warning: Restarts entire swss container.

entries.purpose: NAT manager daemon. Manages NAT/NAPT configuration from CONFIG_DB.
entries.restart_warning: Restarts entire swss container.

entries.purpose: MACsec manager daemon. Manages MACsec encryption configuration from CONFIG_DB.
entries.restart_warning: Restarts entire swss container.

entries.purpose: FRR BGP daemon. Implements BGP-4 protocol: establishes sessions, exchanges routes, runs best-path selection. Managed by bgpcfgd; do not configure directly via vtysh.
entries.restart_warning: Restarts entire bgp container. All BGP sessions will drop.

entries.purpose: FRR Zebra daemon. Central RIB manager for FRR. Receives routes from all FRR protocol daemons, selects best route, programs kernel FIB via netlink, and notifies fpmsyncd via FPM socket.
entries.restart_warning: Restarts entire bgp container.

entries.purpose: FRR static route daemon. Manages static routes configured via FRR and installs them into zebra RIB.
entries.restart_warning: Restarts entire bgp container.

entries.purpose: Unified FRR configuration daemon (alternative to bgpcfgd). Event-driven CONFIG_DB subscriber that supports OSPF, VRFs, IGMP, PIM in addition to BGP. Enabled by setting frr_mgmt_framework_config=true in DEVICE_METADATA.
entries.restart_warning: Restarts entire bgp container.

entries.purpose: FRR OSPF daemon. Implements OSPFv2 protocol.
entries.restart_warning: Restarts entire bgp container.

entries.purpose: FRR OSPFv3 daemon. Implements OSPFv3 for IPv6.
entries.restart_warning: Restarts entire bgp container.

entries.purpose: FRR IS-IS daemon. Implements IS-IS protocol.
entries.restart_warning: Restarts entire bgp container.

entries.purpose: FRR BFD daemon. Implements Bidirectional Forwarding Detection for sub-second failure detection.
entries.restart_warning: Restarts entire bgp container. All BFD sessions will drop, triggering protocol failovers.

entries.purpose: Power Supply Unit daemon. Monitors PSU presence, status (OK/NOT OK), input/output voltage, current, and power via the platform PSU API. Publishes PSU operational data to STATE_DB:PSU_INFO table. Triggers syslog alerts on PSU insertion, removal, or failure events.
entries.restart_warning: Restarting pmon restarts all platform monitoring daemons. No forwarding impact; purely monitoring.

entries.purpose: Thermal control daemon. Reads temperature sensors from the platform thermal API and adjusts fan speeds to maintain safe operating temperatures. Publishes thermal data to STATE_DB:TEMPERATURE_INFO and fan data to STATE_DB:FAN_INFO. Implements thermal policies: if temperature exceeds critical thresholds, triggers shutdown to prevent hardware damage.
entries.restart_warning: Restarting pmon restarts all platform monitoring daemons. Thermal protection is briefly unavailable during restart. No forwarding impact.

entries.purpose: System EEPROM daemon. Reads the chassis EEPROM (TlvInfo format per ONIE specification) at startup and publishes hardware identity data (serial number, MAC address base, platform name, manufacturer) to STATE_DB:EEPROM_INFO table. Runs once at boot; does not poll continuously.
entries.restart_warning: Restarting pmon restarts all platform monitoring daemons. No forwarding impact.

entries.purpose: Inter-Chassis Communication Protocol daemon. Implements ICCP (RFC 7275) for MC-LAG: establishes a TCP session between MC-LAG peers, synchronizes MAC addresses, ARP/NDP entries, and LAG membership state. Detects peer failures via keepalive mechanism and triggers failover to ensure traffic continuity on orphan ports.
entries.restart_warning: Restarting iccpd drops the MC-LAG peer session. All MC-LAG state is lost; peer will detect failure and take over. Traffic disruption on dual-homed hosts until ICCP session re-establishes and state resynchronizes.

entries.purpose: MC-LAG synchronization daemon. Receives synchronized MAC, ARP, and port-channel state from iccpd and writes it to APPL_DB so orchagent can program the ASIC with peer-learned entries. Bridges the ICCP control plane and the SONiC forwarding pipeline.
entries.restart_warning: See iccpd restart warning. mclagsyncd runs in the same container.

entries.purpose: STP manager daemon. Subscribes to CONFIG_DB:STP, STP_VLAN, STP_PORT, and STP_VLAN_PORT tables (bridge priority, port cost, forward delay, hello time) and relays validated configuration to the stpd process, which runs the actual protocol state machines. Part of the swss cfgmgr family but runs inside the stp container.
entries.restart_warning: Restarting the STP container causes all ports to re-enter the STP initialization state. On RSTP networks convergence is fast (~1-2s), but on legacy STP networks ports go through Listening/Learning phases (2x forward-delay, default 30s). Risk of temporary loops during reconvergence.

entries.purpose: STP protocol daemon. Implements the STP/RSTP/PVST+ state machines: sends and receives BPDUs via raw sockets, runs port role selection and topology change handling. Receives configuration from stpmgrd and writes port forwarding state (Forwarding/Blocking/Disabled) to APPL_DB:STP_PORT_TABLE for orchagent to program ASIC port STP state.
entries.restart_warning: See stpmgrd restart warning. stpd runs in the same container; restarting it forces full STP reconvergence on all VLANs.

entries.purpose: FDB synchronization daemon for VXLAN-EVPN. Subscribes to STATE_DB FDB_TABLE (via SubscriberStateTable in fdbsync.cpp) and, when EVPN NVO is configured (CFG_VXLAN_EVPN_NVO_TABLE_NAME), programs learned MACs into the kernel bridge FDB via 'bridge fdb replace' netlink. Also handles VXLAN remote FDB entries (APPL_DB VXLAN_FDB_TABLE) and remote VNI table. Listens on netlink for RTM_NEWNEIGH/RTM_DELNEIGH/RTM_NEWLINK to sync kernel-learned VXLAN FDB back to SONiC tables. Supports Warm Restart via AppRestartAssist.
entries.restart_warning: fdbsyncd runs inside the swss container. Restarting swss restarts orchagent and all *syncd/*mgrd daemons. On EVPN fabrics, restarting causes temporary loss of VXLAN remote MAC/VNI entries until fdbsyncd replays from STATE_DB and netlink.

entries.purpose: Gearbox synchronization daemon. Manages SAI objects for external PHY/retimer (Gearbox) ASICs. Reads from GB_ASIC_DB and calls the Gearbox SAI implementation to program the external PHY. Only present on platforms with external retimer/PHY devices (e.g., certain Memory-port or 400G platforms).
entries.restart_warning: Restarting syncd restarts both syncd and gbsyncd. See syncd restart warning.

entries.purpose: Redis database engine. Hosts all 15 SONiC Redis databases (DB 0-14) in a single Redis instance with logical database separation. Provides the pub/sub notification mechanism that all SONiC daemons use for inter-process communication. The database container is the first to start and the last to stop; all other containers depend on it.
entries.restart_warning: NEVER restart the database container in production. All other SONiC containers depend on Redis. Restarting it destroys all volatile DB state (APPL_DB, ASIC_DB, STATE_DB, COUNTERS_DB) and causes every daemon to lose its communication channel. Equivalent to a cold reboot without the clean startup sequence.
